tools.name: watcher_create
tools.description: Create a new watcher that polls an external service for events and processes them with an action prompt

name.description: A human-readable name for this watcher (e.g. "My Gmail")

action_prompt.description: Instructions for the LLM on how to handle detected events. This prompt is sent along with event data to a background conversation.

poll_interval_ms.description: How often to poll in milliseconds. Defaults to 60000 (1 minute). Minimum 15000.

credential_service.description: Override the credential service to use. Defaults to the provider's required service.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: watcher_list
tools.description: List all watchers with their status, or show details for a specific watcher

watcher_id.description: If provided, show detailed info for this specific watcher including recent events.

enabled_only.description: When true, only show enabled watchers. Defaults to false.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: watcher_update
tools.description: Update a watcher's configuration (name, action prompt, interval, enabled state)

watcher_id.description: The ID of the watcher to update

action_prompt.description: New action prompt for event processing

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: watcher_delete
tools.description: Delete a watcher and all its event history

watcher_id.description: The ID of the watcher to delete

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: watcher_digest
tools.description: Get a summary of recent watcher activity. Use this when the user asks about what happened with their email, notifications, etc.

watcher_id.description: Filter to events from a specific watcher. If omitted, shows events from all watchers.

hours.description: How many hours back to look. Defaults to 24.

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return. Defaults to 50.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called
